bert_explanations.explanation:
[PREDICTION]: BOTNET (Attack) | Confidence: 90.3%
[FLOW DESCRIPTION]: Network flow characteristics: Bwd Pkt Len Std is high (1.86); Init Fwd Win Byts = 1.69; Fwd Pkt Len Std is high (1.50); Fwd Seg Size Min = 1.32; Pkt Len Std is high (1.28); Fwd Pkt Len Max is high (1.24); Bwd Pkt Len Max is high (1.24); Pkt Len Max is high (1.22); PSH Flag Cnt count is 1.2; Bwd Seg Size Avg = 1.05.
[REASON]: The network flow matches Botnet patterns. We observed high-rate packet flooding and/or brute force attempts (e.g., FTP/SSH Brute Force, DoS-Hulk, DoS-GoldenEye). The hybrid fusion model flagged this anomalous traffic using both tabular signature features and text representation semantics processed by DistilBERT.
[ATTACK TYPE]: Botnet (Botnet)
[RECOMMENDATION]: Immediate action required. Isolate the affected IoT device. Block the source IP on the firewall. Update SSH/FTP credentials immediately. Review traffic rate limiting policies.

[PREDICTION]: BENIGN | Confidence: 61.2%
[FLOW DESCRIPTION]: Network flow characteristics: PSH Flag Cnt count is 1.2; Down/Up Ratio = -0.94; ACK Flag Cnt count is 0.7; Fwd Act Data Pkts is high (0.56); Pkt Len Min is low (-0.54); Protocol = -0.51; Fwd Pkt Len Min is low (-0.51); flow duration is short; Bwd Pkt Len Min is low (-0.50); Fwd Seg Size Avg = 0.49.
[REASON]: The flow characteristics are consistent with benign IoT communication. Packet lengths, transmission rates, and protocol flag frequencies are within normal operating bounds.
[ATTACK TYPE]: N/A
[RECOMMENDATION]: No action required. Continue monitoring traffic logs.

[PREDICTION]: MALWARE (Attack) | Confidence: 98.0%
[FLOW DESCRIPTION]: Network flow characteristics: Fwd Seg Size Min = 2.20; Init Fwd Win Byts = 1.69; PSH Flag Cnt count is 1.2; Down/Up Ratio = -0.94; Fwd Pkt Len Mean is low (-0.91); Fwd Seg Size Avg = -0.91; Pkt Size Avg is low (-0.79); Pkt Len Max is low (-0.75); Pkt Len Std is low (-0.74); Bwd Pkt Len Max is low (-0.72).
[REASON]: The network flow matches Malware patterns. We observed slow-rate exhaustion or application exploits (e.g., DoS-Slowloris, DoS-SlowHTTPTest). Unlike high-rate Botnet flooding, this traffic uses low packet frequency but holds sockets open, which the hybrid model detected via temporal features and text embeddings.
[ATTACK TYPE]: Malware (Malware)
[RECOMMENDATION]: Immediate action required. Terminate active slow connections. Configure Web Server timeout settings (e.g., RequestReadTimeout). Deploy a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter slow-rate connection hijacking.
